framework.name: Pi Agent
framework.description: Layered TypeScript monorepo — strict 4-layer architecture (pi-ai → pi-agent-core → pi-coding-agent → pi-tui). Explicit context control, self-extensible tool system, ReAct double-loop with human-in-the-loop steering. Built by Mario Zechner (badlogic) as a 'factory for building AI assistants'.

modules.name: pi-ai (Foundation)
modules.description: Unified LLM API for 20+ providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, xAI, Groq, Ollama, vLLM). TypeBox tool schemas. Context serialization and provider-native message conversion. Zero dependencies on upper layers.

modules.name: pi-agent-core (Agent Runtime)
modules.description: Generic ReAct agent runtime: double-loop design (outer steering loop + inner tool-call loop). State machine, event streaming, parallel/sequential tool execution. transformContext + convertToLlm hooks for explicit control.

modules.name: pi-coding-agent (CLI)
modules.description: Interactive CLI coding agent. Session persistence, custom tool sets, theme engine, hooks. Loads project context from AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md. Self-extensible — agent can modify its own tools and prompts.

modules.name: pi-tui (Terminal UI)
modules.description: Retained-mode differential rendering terminal UI. Markdown rendering, autocomplete editors, streaming tool output display. Subscribes to agent event stream for real-time updates.

modules.name: Context Transform Hook
modules.description: Plugin point: optional pruning, compaction, or retrieval injection before each turn. Gives developers full control over what enters the model's context window.

modules.name: Tool Executor
modules.description: Executes tool calls from agent loop. Supports parallel (default) and sequential modes. Emits typed events: tool_execution_start/update/end.

modules.name: Session Store
modules.description: Persistent conversation sessions. Stores AgentMessage[] history, system prompt, tool definitions. Enables session suspend/resume across CLI restarts.

modules.name: pi-mom (Slack Bot)
modules.description: Slack integration — connects Pi Agent as a bot in Slack workspaces. Channels messages through same agent loop, delivers responses back to threads.

modules.name: pi-pods (GPU Manager)
modules.description: Remote GPU pod manager for self-hosted vLLM deployment. Provisions and manages inference infrastructure independently of the agent loop.

metadata.description: CLI receives user input → delegates to Agent Core for ReAct loop execution
Core sends provider-native messages; pi-ai returns streaming or complete response
Before each turn: optional pruning/compaction/retrieval injection via transformContext hook
Model returns tool calls → Tool Executor runs in parallel or sequential mode → results flow back
Save/load AgentMessage[] history + system prompt for session continuity
Typed event stream (agent_start → turn_start → text_* → toolcall_* → tool_exec_* → turn_end → agent_end)
Inbound Slack messages → agent loop → response back to Slack thread
Provision/stop remote GPU pods for self-hosted vLLM inference
pi-ai routes inference requests to remote vLLM pods when using self-hosted models
Tool execution results appended to session history for context continuity
Agent response delivered back to Slack channel via pi-mom

phases.name: Context Preparation
phases.description: Load session, transform context, convert to provider-native format

steps.description: CLI receives user input; loads AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md project context
Restore previous AgentMessage[] history and system prompt if resuming session
Optional hook: prune old messages, inject retrieved context, compact if needed

phases.name: ReAct Double Loop
phases.description: Outer loop (steering) → Inner loop (tool calls). Thought → Action → Observation cycle with event streaming.

steps.description: Outer while(true): manages steering/follow-up with human-in-the-loop intervention
Convert messages to provider-native format; stream response: thinking + text + tool calls
Inner while(hasMoreToolCalls): Thought → Action → Observation. Emits typed events.
Run tool calls in parallel (default) or sequential mode; emit start/update/end events
TUI subscribes to event stream; differential rendering updates display in real-time
After tool results: model decides — more tools, or final answer (terminate: true)
Append tool results and response to session history for future turns

phases.name: Response Delivery
phases.description: Format final answer and deliver to user

steps.description: Model emits final text without tool calls; inner loop terminates
Final answer rendered in terminal via markdown-aware differential display
Complete session saved for suspend/resume on next CLI invocation
